chain.thought: Explanation: The youngest age at the start date is 49 years, 241 days for Theodore Roosevelt when he became the oldest living president on June 24, 1908. No other president listed had a younger start age.

judge_thought: Explanation: The table lists presidents when they became the oldest living president, with their age at the start date. Interpreting "youngest living president" as the youngest age at the start of being the oldest living president, the ages show Theodore Roosevelt was the youngest at 49 years, 241 days (on June 24, 1908), which is the lowest age in the column.
